channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Analyst Reacts to Trump’s Latest Threats Against Iran on Social Media
item.description: In a recent social media post, former President Donald Trump made bold statements about escalating tensions with Iran. He threatened further strikes on Iranian infrastructure, which sparked a reaction...

item.title: Air India CEO Campbell Wilson Resigns, Transition Period Announced
item.description: Air India's chief executive, Campbell Wilson, has resigned from his position but will be staying on with the airline to ensure a smooth transition until a replacement is found. This development comes ...

item.title: Mbalula Addresses ANC Trademark Dispute, Service Delivery Concerns at Mahlangu Commemoration
item.description: ANC Secretary-General Fikile Mbalula recently led commemorations honouring anti-apartheid activist Solomon Mahlangu, marking the anniversary of his execution. The event included visits to the prison w...

item.title: Two orphaned bear cubs find refuge at Vancouver Island wildlife centre after mother’s death
item.description: Two young black bear cubs from Vancouver Island have found shelter at the North Island Wildlife Recovery Centre after their mother was discovered dead inside their den. According to the centre’s state...

item.title: Arslanbek Makhmudov on faith, family and facing Tyson Fury at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium
item.description: In an interview ahead of his heavyweight bout with Tyson Fury at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium, Arslanbek Makhmudov speaks warmly and respectfully of the former world champion, describing him as the ‘prof...

item.title: Ministry clarifies support issue as Kenyan skater Kevin Kiarie shines in Benin championship
item.description: Kenyan speed skater Kevin Kiarie has become the centre of online discussion after his impressive win in Benin. His victory brought pride to many Kenyans, who flooded social media with praise and quest...

item.title: Severe Tropical Cyclone Maila may strike Far North Queensland after Narelle's impact
item.description: A second tropical cyclone could strike far north Queensland just weeks after Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle caused significant damage to the region. The Bureau of Meteorology (BoM) has issued warning...

item.title: UCLA and Cal State Universities Receive $110 Million to Address Mental Health Worker Shortage
item.description: UCLA, Cal State L.A., and Cal State Dominguez Hills have been granted $110 million by the Ballmer Group to expand mental health programs, offer scholarships, and fill the workforce gap in social work ...

item.title: Trump Calls for Iranians to Protest Government if Ceasefire is Implemented
item.description: In the ongoing conflict surrounding Iran, former U.S. President Donald Trump has made a bold statement urging the Iranian people to rise against their government if a ceasefire is declared. Trump's co...

item.title: Kenya government backs local electric car production amid fuel crisis
item.description: The Kenyan government is now fast-tracking plans to embrace locally assembled electric vehicles as part of its response to the escalating fuel crisis that has gripped the country in recent weeks. The ...

item.title: Family Moves Into Unfinished House to Save on Rent While Trusting God for Completion
item.description: A Kenyan woman recently shared her family's decision to move into their unfinished house, which still lacks a toilet, in order to save on rent. The house, constructed with uncut stones and rough walls...

item.title: Police investigate death of man in Glasgow’s Castlemilk district
item.description: Police Scotland has begun a murder investigation following the death of Craig Hamilton, a 42‑year‑old man found dead at a property in Glasgow’s Castlemilk area. Officers were called to Drakemire Avenu...

item.title: Charli XCX’s team clarifies songwriting credits after Sky Ferreira’s concerns over ‘Wuthering Heights’ soundtrack
item.description: Charli XCX’s management has issued a statement responding to recent claims by Sky Ferreira that parts of the soundtrack for Emerald Fennell’s film Wuthering Heights were based on her old music demos. ...

item.title: Peter Kaluma Criticises UDA Aspirants Amid ODM Zoning Debate
item.description: Homa Bay Town MP Peter Kaluma has sparked controversy after labelling several UDA aspirants in Nyanza as 'taka taka', meaning rubbish. He was responding to a list of 27 UDA candidates contesting for p...

item.title: Woman arrested for hiding firearms under mattress during police raid
item.description: A 36-year-old woman from the Free State was arrested after attempting to conceal four rifles and an airgun beneath her mattress during a police operation. The incident took place on Sunday evening in ...

item.title: Fifty-eight couples celebrate marriage at IPHC Easter ceremony
item.description: During the Easter weekend, 58 couples celebrated their marriages at the International Pentecost Holiness Church (IPHC), where His Grace Comforter FLG Modise officiated the ceremonies. The event, held ...

item.title: Odisha Governor Fills Long-Vacant Vice-Chancellor Posts in 14 State Universities
item.description: On Monday, Odisha Governor Hari Babu Kambhampati appointed vice-chancellors for 14 state universities, ending long-standing vacancies in higher education leadership. These universities, including Utka...

item.title: Development and agriculture concerns overshadowed by personality politics in Bagalkot bypoll campaign
item.description: As Bagalkot in Karnataka approaches its bypolls, the fortnight-long campaign has largely revolved around personal attacks among political leaders, sidelining key regional issues. Despite the district’...

item.title: Squamish First Nation and Climbers Criticize Volkswagen Beetle Prank on Sacred Rock Face
item.description: A prank involving a red Volkswagen Beetle hanging from the rock face of The Papoose, a popular climbing spot along the Sea to Sky Highway, has sparked strong backlash. The Squamish Nation, who view th...

item.title: Artemis II Astronauts to Break Space Distance Record and Advance Moon Exploration
item.description: Four astronauts aboard NASA’s Artemis II mission don do something no human ever try before — they don fly deeper into space, going past the distance Apollo 13 reach back in 1972. The crew, led by Comm...
